---
title: "Nonparametric & Semiparametric Methods"
subtitle: "Letting the data choose the functional form"
---

## Overview

Every regression so far committed to a functional form --- a line, a logit curve, a polynomial. But what if you do not want to assume the shape? Nonparametric methods estimate distributions and conditional means directly from the data, imposing smoothness rather than a specific equation, and letting the curve bend wherever the data say it should.

This module develops the core ideas. Kernel density estimation smooths a distribution without bins; kernel and local-polynomial regression fit a flexible conditional mean (the very machinery behind regression discontinuity and LOESS); and series/spline methods turn flexibility back into a tractable linear regression on basis functions. Throughout, one parameter --- the bandwidth or the number of terms --- governs the bias-variance tradeoff, and choosing it well is the whole game. The module closes on semiparametric models that combine a parametric part of interest with a nonparametric nuisance, the conceptual bridge to double machine learning.

## Pages in This Module

- [Kernel Density Estimation](kernel-density.qmd) --- Estimating a distribution without assuming its shape.
- [Kernel and Local Polynomial Regression](kernel-local-regression.qmd) --- Letting the data choose the curve.
- [Series and Spline Methods](series-splines.qmd) --- Flexible curves from basis functions.
